name: From Macedo de Cavaleiros to the Albufeira do Azibo beach — circular
name_original: De Macedo de Cavaleiros para o praia Albufeira do Azibo — circular
region_intro: Road cycling around Macedo De Cavaleiros features routes that navigate through a landscape characterized by rolling hills, agricultural areas, and the scenic Albufeira do Azibo reservoir. The region offers varied terrain, including climbs with significant elevation gain, making it suitable for cyclists seeking a challenge. Routes often pass through rural settings and offer views of local landmarks and natural features. The area provides a network of roads connecting towns and natural attractions, ideal for extended rides. Best road cycling routes around Macedo De Cavaleiros The most popular road cycling route is Macedo de Cavaleiros e Albufeira do Azibo — circular o Paisagem do Morais, a 25.4 miles (40.9 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular path around the Albufeira do Azibo and through the Morais landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Macedo de Cavaleiros to the Albufeira do Azibo beach — circular, a difficult 43.5 miles (69.9 km) path. This route provides a challenging ride to the Albufeira do Azibo beach, featuring significant elevation changes and scenic views. Local road cyclists also love the Trindade Coelho Statue – View of Mogadouro Castle loop from Macedo de Cavaleiros, a 60.4 miles (97.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views towards Mogadouro Castle, often completed in about 4 hours 54 minutes. Road cycling around Macedo De Cavaleiros is defined by rolling hills, reservoir views, and rural roads, offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Macedo De Cavaleiros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 99 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Macedo De Cavaleiros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Macedo De Cavaleiros?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes available around Macedo De Cavaleiros, offering diverse experiences for cyclists of varying skill levels.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Macedo De Cavaleiros?
A: While the region features rolling hills, many routes are classified as moderate, making them accessible for beginners looking for a pleasant ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32144784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Macedo de Cavaleiros loop from Macedo de Cavaleiros&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Macedo De Cavaleiros?
A: Road cycling around Macedo De Cavaleiros is characterized by rolling hills, agricultural areas, and scenic views, particularly around the Albufeira do Azibo reservoir. You'll encounter varied terrain, including climbs with significant elevation gain on some routes.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, experienced cyclists will find plenty of challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1401105172/from-macedo-de-cavaleiros-to-the-albufeira-do-azibo-beach-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Macedo de Cavaleiros to the Albufeira do Azibo beach — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 43.5-mile path with significant elevation changes, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24551085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trindade Coelho Statue – View of Mogadouro Castle loop from Macedo de Cavaleiros&lt;/a&gt; offers a 60.4-mile ride with varied terrain.

Q: What scenic attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of natural and cultural highlights. You might pass by the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4886151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufeira do Azibo&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6445599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geopark of Macedo de Cavaleiros&lt;/a&gt;, or even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7459025" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murals of Podence&lt;/a&gt;. The region's rural settings provide constant scenic backdrops.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1401104092/macedo-de-cavaleiros-e-albufeira-do-azibo-circular-o-paisagem-do-morais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Macedo de Cavaleiros e Albufeira do Azibo — circular o Paisagem do Morais&lt;/a&gt;, which loops around the Albufeira do Azibo.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Macedo De Cavaleiros?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic views of the Albufeira do Azibo, and the quiet rural roads that connect towns and natural attractions.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24551085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trindade Coelho Statue – View of Mogadouro Castle loop from Macedo de Cavaleiros&lt;/a&gt; cover nearly 60 miles (97 km), offering an extended cycling experience through varied landscapes.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by bodies of water?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4886151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufeira do Azibo&lt;/a&gt; reservoir is a prominent feature in the region, and many road cycling routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1401104092/macedo-de-cavaleiros-e-albufeira-do-azibo-circular-o-paisagem-do-morais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Macedo de Cavaleiros e Albufeira do Azibo — circular o Paisagem do Morais&lt;/a&gt;, offer beautiful views of its waters and even pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6342785" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufeira do Azibo Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Macedo De Cavaleiros?
A: The region generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling during spring and autumn, with milder temperatures. Summers can be warm, while winters are cooler but still offer opportunities for dedicated cyclists, though some routes might be less frequented.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the local cultural heritage?
A: While primarily focused on natural landscapes, some routes may pass through villages where you can experience local culture. For example, you might encounter the traditional &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7459024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caretos House&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7459025" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murals of Podence&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's unique heritage.
tips:
With the administrative reform of 1853, Macedo de Cavaleiros became one of the municipalities of Portugal. This elevation to the status of municipality was met with opposition, both in rural areas, where the struggles between those in favour of the old and the new municipality lasted until 1911, and in parliamentary circles, triggered mainly by the deputy for the Moncorvo constituency, João Pedro de Almeida Pessanha. Even so, the municipality was confirmed by royal decree on 24 October 1855.

On 15 January 1863, Macedo de Cavaleiros was elevated to the status of town.

In 1903, the railway was built, establishing the Tua line, which naturally gave a strong economic boost to the region.

Finally, on 13 May 1999, Macedo de Cavaleiros was elevated to the status of city.

Praia Fluvial da Fraga da Pegada is a river beach located in the Albufeira do Azibo, near Macedo de Cavaleiros, in the Trás-os-Montes region (northern Portugal). It is one of the most highly regarded destinations for nature tourism and water sports in the area.
